summary: Fixed the strict web typecheck regressions while preserving the real Chromium Dedicated Worker and packaged Electron renderer/Worker host-hash canaries. The browser canary now uses the existing worker-compatible adapter path without adding a direct web dependency on @monsoon/sim-core, the DedicatedWorker typing issue is resolved without weakening strictness, and the host-hash comparison still prints the Node baseline plus Chromium/Electron hashes for hello, command/query, and save/load.

tests_run.result: Worktree remains dirty only in the intended canary and orchestration paths. Newly written QA review and writer review handoff files are present.
tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json passed after removing the direct web package dependency on @monsoon/sim-core and replacing the worker adapter TLA with static imports.
ESLint passed for apps/web source and vite.config.ts.

Node hash 61e0ba1c matched Worker-compatible hash 61e0ba1c; command/query hash d275faa4 matched Worker-compatible command/query hash d275faa4; save/load hash 28074edd matched Worker-compatible save/load hash 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Vite built dist/index.html and dist/host-hash-canary.html plus the host-hash-canary worker bundle and shared assets.
Electron security preferences passed; preload exposes only the named monsoonDesktop bridge; renderer file navigation is restricted to the packaged renderer root.
Packaged main, preload, renderer, and platform adapter artifacts to .tmp/desktop-package/MonsoonSovereigns-win32-x64.

Command: pnpm --filter @monsoon/sim-runner sim:host-hash-canary -- --browser chromium. Chromium dedicated worker matched Node baseline hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.

Command: pnpm --filter @monsoon/sim-runner sim:host-hash-canary -- --browser electron. Electron renderer dedicated worker matched Node baseline hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.

decisions: Removed the direct workspace dependency on @monsoon/sim-core from apps/web so the browser package typechecks without traversing sim-core source through the web project.
Kept the browser canary on the existing worker-compatible adapter path, which still exercises the real deterministic simulation logic while staying inside the browser bundle constraints.
Replaced the top-level await import in apps/web/src/worker/hello-simulation-adapter.mjs with static imports so Vite can bundle the Dedicated Worker path.
Kept Electron security unchanged: nodeIntegration remains false, the preload bridge stays narrow, and the canary is activated only by MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY=1.
risks: apps/web/src/worker/hello-simulation-adapter.mjs still imports sim-core source indirectly, so future type or bundler changes in that adapter should be checked against the canary build path.
The desktop packaging step still depends on a successful web build so the packaged renderer can include the canary page.
The repo remains dirty in the lead-owned orchestration files that were already present before this task.
